questions.text: The term Auxin was coined by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect.
The term auxin is derived from the Greek word auxein which means to grow. In 1928, Frits Warmolt Went coined the term auxin.

questions.text: A plant reponse to touch is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Photoperiodism is the response to changes in daylength that enables plants to adapt to seasonal changes in their environment.
Geotropism (also called gravitropism) is the directional growth of an organism in response to gravity.
Thigmotropism is when a plant responds to a movement or touch from another stimulus.
In biology, phototropism is the growth of an organism in response to a light stimulus.

questions.explanation: Thigmotropism is when a plant responds to a movement or touch from another stimulus.

questions.text: The biological clock is time measuring system which is independent and is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Biological clock is endogenous.
Biological clock is not exogenous
This option is incorrect.
This option is incorrect.

questions.text: If bio-rhythm occurs with a frequency of 24 hours, it is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Circadian (Latin circa =about, dies =day) means about one day.
If the biorhythms are of about 365 days, these rhythms in activity are called circannual.
Lunar rhythms have a periodicity of 29.53 days, as exemplified by the recurrence of the full moon every month.

options.text: All of the choices are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Circadian (Latin circa =about, dies =day) means about one day.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It promotes closing of stomata under conditions of water stress (wilting).
This option is incorrect.

questions.text: The production of fructants by plants is a response to:
questions.subject: Biology

questions.text: The formation of air tubes in submerged roots is an adaptation to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Responses of plants to water stress may be assigned as either injurious change or tolerance index.
The formation of air tubes in submerged roots occur due to oxygen deprivation.

In response to salt stress, plants adapt via various mechanisms, including regulating ion homeostasis, activating the osmotic stress pathway, mediating plant hormone signaling, and regulating cytoskeleton dynamics and the cell wall composition.
Plants respond to herbivore attack through an intricate and dynamic defense system that includes structural barriers, toxic chemicals, and attraction of natural enemies of the target pests.

questions.explanation: The formation of air tubes in submerged roots occur due to oxygen deprivation.

questions.text: The first line of defense against pathogen is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phytoalexins are antimicrobial substances of low molecular weight produced by plants in response to infection or stress, which form part of their active defense mechanisms.
Cellulose cell walls form a physical barrier between the cells and pathogens, thus protect against infection.

questions.explanation: Cellulose cell walls form a physical barrier between the cells and pathogens, thus protect against infection.

questions.text: Auxin causes:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Auxins promote apical dominance by suppressing the lateral growth of axillary buds.
Adventitious root (AR) formation is a complex developmental process controlled by a plethora of endogenous and environmental factors and Auxin is well known to regulate adventitious root development.
Auxin controls many aspects of fruit development, including fruit set and growth, ripening and abscission.

options.text: All of the choices are correct
options.explanation: Auxin is a plant hormone responsible for growth nd development, helps in cell elongation of stems and roots, and promotes flowering.

questions.explanation: Auxin is a plant hormone responsible for growth nd development, helps in cell elongation of stems and roots, and promotes flowering.

questions.text: In combination with auxin,it stimulates cell division in plants and determines the course of differentiation:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ethylene is regarded as a multifunctional phytohormone that regulates both growth, and senescence. It promotes or inhibits growth and senescence processes depending on its concentration, timing of application, and the plant species.
Gibberellin (GA) is one of the plant hormones that regulate a wide range of processes involved in plant growth, organ development, and environmental responses.
Abscisic acid is a plant hormone that regulates growth, development, and stress responses within a plant, specifically through the inhibition of cell division in plant cells.
Cytokinins are a group of plant growth regulators which are primarily involved in performing cell division in plant roots, shoot system.

questions.explanation: Cytokinins are a group of plant growth regulators which are primarily involved in performing cell division in plant roots, shoot system.

questions.text: Foolish seedling disease in rice is caused by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ethylene is regarded as a multifunctional phytohormone that regulates both growth, and senescence. It promotes or inhibits growth and senescence processes depending on its concentration, timing of application, and the plant species.

Bakanae or “foolish seedling” is a common seedling disease in rice caused by the fungus Gibberellafujikuroi. Infected seedlings exhibit abnormal elongation and in severe cases stunting leading to death. It led to the discovery of GA.
Abscisic acid is a plant hormone that regulates growth, development, and stress responses within a plant, specifically through the inhibition of cell division in plant cells.
Cytokinins are a group of plant growth regulators which are primarily involved in performing cell division in plant roots, shoot system.

questions.explanation: Bakanae or “foolish seedling” is a common seedling disease in rice caused by the fungus Gibberellafujikuroi. Infected seedlings exhibit abnormal elongation and in severe cases stunting leading to death. It led to the discovery of GA.

questions.text: One of the most important uses of auxin is the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The abscission process begins when plants detect seasonal changes, including decreased sunlight, a reduction in chlorophyll, the generation of chemicals due to stress, and hormones.
Ethylene also induces the expression of at least some genes encoding pathogenesis-related proteins.
Auxin delays abscission of young leaves and fruits. Its effect is through non-formation of abscission zone below a leaf or fruit. Abscission zone cuts off nutrients and water supply.
Ethylene accelerates the rate of abscission in many plants by inducing the synthesis of wall hydrolases.

questions.explanation: Auxin delays abscission of young leaves and fruits. Its effect is through non-formation of abscission zone below a leaf or fruit. Abscission zone cuts off nutrients and water supply.

questions.text: Abscisic acid:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: ABA is the growth inhibitor hormone in plants. It induces the dormancy of buds.

Abscisic acid (ABA) is a stress hormone that accumulates under different abiotic and biotic stresses. A typical effect of ABA on leaves is to reduce transpirational water loss by closing stomata and parallelly defend against microbes by restricting their entry through stomatal pores.
Abscisic acid (ABA) largely promotes leaf senescence and inhibits seed germination in plants.

options.text: All of the choices are correct
options.explanation: Abscisic acid (ABA) is a plant hormone that regulates numerous aspects of plant growth, development, and stress responses.

questions.explanation: Abscisic acid (ABA) is a plant hormone that regulates numerous aspects of plant growth, development, and stress responses.

questions.text: Which of the following is weedicide hormone:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Auxin herbicides stimulate a variety of growth and developmental processes when present at low concentrations at the cellular sites of action. However, with increasing concentration and auxin activity in the tissue, growth is disturbed and the plant is lethally damaged.
Gibberellin (GA) is one of the plant hormones that regulate a wide range of processes involved in plant growth, organ development, and environmental responses.
Abscisic acid is a plant hormone that regulates growth, development, and stress responses within a plant, specifically through the inhibition of cell division in plant cells.

Ethylene is regarded as a multifunctional phytohormone that regulates both growth, and senescence. It promotes or inhibits growth and senescence processes depending on its concentration, timing of application, and the plant species.

questions.explanation: Auxin herbicides stimulate a variety of growth and developmental processes when present at low concentrations at the cellular sites of action. However, with increasing concentration and auxin activity in the tissue, growth is disturbed and the plant is lethally damaged.

questions.text: Gibberellic acid was discovered by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gibberellin was first discovered in 1935 by Teijiro Yabuta and Hayashi. It was isolated from fungus Gibberella fujikuroi and hence isolate was given gibberellin name. It is a plant growth regulator. It is involved in various processes like stem elongation, germination, dormancy, leaf and fruit senescence, etc.

questions.explanation: Gibberellin was first discovered in 1935 by Teijiro Yabuta and Hayashi. It was isolated from fungus Gibberella fujikuroi and hence isolate was given gibberellin name. It is a plant growth regulator. It is involved in various processes like stem elongation, germination, dormancy, leaf and fruit senescence, etc.

questions.text: Plants may be made to grow taller by applying the chemical:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: 2,4-Dichlorophenoxyacetic acid (2,4-D) is a common systemic herbicide used in the control of broadleaf weeds.
It has a role as a defoliant, a synthetic auxin and a phenoxy herbicide.
It stimulates cell division and expansion in response to light or dark.

questions.explanation: It stimulates cell division and expansion in response to light or dark.

questions.text: Ethene:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ethylene is a gaseous hormone that plays an important role in fruit ripening.
Ethylene causes apical hook formation in dicot seedlings, promotes senescence and abscission of plant leaves and flowers.
Ethylene also helps in the initiation of flowering and it also initiates germination.

options.text: All of the choices are correct
options.explanation: Ethylene is regarded as a multifunctional phytohormone that regulates both growth, and senescence. It promotes or inhibits growth and senescence processes depending on its concentration, timing of application, and the plant species.

questions.explanation: Ethylene is regarded as a multifunctional phytohormone that regulates both growth, and senescence. It promotes or inhibits growth and senescence processes depending on its concentration, timing of application, and the plant species.

questions.text: The hormone responsible for delay of senescence is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ethylene is regarded as a multifunctional phytohormone that regulates both growth, and senescence. It promotes or inhibits growth and senescence processes depending on its concentration, timing of application, and the plant species.

Gibberellins (GAs) are plant hormones that regulate various developmental processes, including stem elongation, germination, dormancy, flowering, flower development, and leaf and fruit senescence. GAs are one of the longest-known classes of plant hormone.

Abscisic acid is a hormone that suppresses the growth of plants. It does so by counteracting the hormones that promote the growth of plants. The other functions of this hormone are maintaining seed dormancy, causing wilting of leaves, etc.

Cytokinin is a plant hormone. It plays major role in cell proliferation and cell division. During senescence, cytokinin levels are low. It delays in senescence of leaves when sprayed externally by delaying the destruction of chlorophyll and proteins in leaves.

questions.explanation: Cytokinin is a plant hormone. It plays major role in cell proliferation and cell division. During senescence, cytokinin levels are low. It delays in senescence of leaves when sprayed externally by delaying the destruction of chlorophyll and proteins in leaves.

questions.text: The naturally occurring cytokinin is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Zeatin is a plant growth hormone in the cytokinin family. Zeatin was discovered in immature corn kernels.
Kinetin was the first artificial cytokinin to be discovered.
NAA is a synthetic plant hormone in the auxin family.
Indole-3-butyric acid (1H-indole-3-butanoic acid, IBA) is a plant hormone in the auxin family and is an ingredient in many commercial horticultural plant rooting products.

questions.explanation: Zeatin is a plant growth hormone in the cytokinin family. Zeatin was discovered in immature corn kernels.

questions.text: Gibberellin:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gibberellin (GA) is one of the plant hormones that regulate a wide range of processes involved in plant growth, organ development, and environmental responses. These include seed germination, stem elongation, leaf expansion, transition to flowering, and the development of flowers, fruits, and seeds.

questions.explanation: Gibberellin (GA) is one of the plant hormones that regulate a wide range of processes involved in plant growth, organ development, and environmental responses. These include seed germination, stem elongation, leaf expansion, transition to flowering, and the development of flowers, fruits, and seeds.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT function of auxin?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Hormones, auxins, gibberellins, and cytokinins, especially the first two, are well known to induce parthenocarpy.
Apical dominance is the phenomenon which is exhibited by auxin hormone. Apical dominance results in the suppression of growth of lateral buds.
Auxin delays abscission of young leaves and fruits. Its effect is through non-formation of abscission zone below a leaf or fruit. Abscission zone cuts off nutrients and water supply.

options.text: All of the choices are correct

questions.explanation: Auxin delays abscission of young leaves and fruits. Its effect is through non-formation of abscission zone below a leaf or fruit. Abscission zone cuts off nutrients and water supply.
